title: UDesigner Visual Oracle Verdict
description: Structured output from the Visual Lane — a read-only vision agent that evaluates rendered design screenshots.

reference.description: Reference URL or image path, if provided

scores.description: Rubric scores 1-10 (higher = better, except ai_slop_smell where lower = better)

first_impression_impact.description: 5-second visual impact for a senior designer. Is the first screen memorable, not generic?

composition_balance.description: Visual weight distribution across sections. Are section transitions thoughtful? Is the eye guided?

typography_drama.description: Type hierarchy confidence — scale contrast, tracking, single-word emphasis, font pairing quality

color_harmony.description: Palette coherence, contrast choices, accent restraint, saturation discipline

ai_slop_smell.description: Inverse scale — 1 = pristine, 10 = reeks of AI defaults (3-equal bento, center everything, purple-blue gradient, generic icons)

agency_tier_similarity.description: How close to Agency tier (journey-digital, pagebreak) or Tech tier (Vercel, Framer, Stripe) standards

composite_visual.description: Aggregated 0-100 visual score. Formula: 10*(impact + composition + typography + color + (11 - ai_slop_smell) + agency_tier) / 6

critique.description: Specific issues, 1-5 bullet points. Each must name a concrete section and a concrete defect.

section.description: Section name as visible in the design (e.g., 'Hero', 'Pricing grid')

issue.description: Concrete description of the defect (NOT vague — must reference specific visual attribute)

weakest_section.description: Single section name that most drags the composite down

suggested_fix.description: ONE targeted fix. Must be actionable (section + change), never vague.

change.description: Exact change instruction (e.g., 'Hero title: increase from text-5xl to text-7xl, add italic emphasis on single word')

semantic_match.description: How closely the vibe matches the reference (not pixels — aesthetic DNA)

divergences.description: Named aspects where the output meaningfully differs from the reference
